name: 2-minute Fudge
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 1 1/2 quart casserole dish, stir together the confectioners' sugar, cocoa, salt, milk, and vanilla until partially blended (the mixture will be too stiff to thoroughly blend in all of the dry ingredients).
2. Place butter over top in the center of the dish.
3. Microwave at High (10) for 2 minutes. Stir vigorously until smooth. If all of the butter has not melted during cooking, it will melt as the mixture is stirred.
4. Blend in the nuts.
5. Pour into a wax paper-lined 8 x 8 x 2-inch dish.
6. Chill for 1 hour in the refrigerator or 20 to 30 minutes in the freezer.
7. Cut into squares. Makes about 36 squares.
Peanut Butter Fudge: Follow the directions above, except substitute 1/2 cup crunchy peanut butter for cocoa and add chopped peanuts.
Coffee Fondant: Prepare the 2-Minute Fudge as above, omitting cocoa and substituting 1 tablespoon instant coffee granules dissolved in 2 tablespoons milk. Before the fondant sets, press chocolate shots decoratively into the top.
